title: Baby Scratch
description: Learn the foundational scratching technique — move the record forward and backward in rhythm while the fader stays open.

steps.instruction: Watch: the jog wheel moves forward — a short push. You hear a brief forward sound.
Watch: the jog wheel pulls back — same distance. You hear the reverse. Forward and back — that is a baby scratch.
Watch: the movement repeats in rhythm — forward, back, forward, back. The scratch has a pulse.

phases.instruction: Imagine you are swiping a finger across a vinyl record — forward, then back. Feel the rhythm of that motion. It is not random wiggles; it has a steady tempo. Each forward-back is one beat. Two forward-backs per bar if you scratch on the 8th notes.

steps.instruction: Load a vocal sample or a track with a clear sound on Deck A. Play it so you have audio to scratch.
Place your hand on the jog wheel. Push it forward about half an inch — then pull it back the same distance. Do this slowly first.
Now add rhythm. Push forward on beat 1, pull back on the and. Keep the motion even — equal distance each direction.
Speed up slightly. Try to scratch in time with the beat of Deck B if it is playing, or at a steady tempo.
The baby scratch sounds best when it is even and rhythmic. Keep practicing — equal motion, steady tempo.
